title: TODO comment — file NOT migrated to withRoute HOF
description: The file has an explicit TODO 'Phase 1: migrate the whole file to withRoute HOF + rbac callback' but is still a raw NextApiHandler. PATCH/DELETE use manual getServerSession + manual RBAC (member lookup with hardcoded permissions) and manual rate-limit via checkRateLimit(ip). Inconsistent envelope shape ({error: '...'}), missing Sentry.capture, missing audit log on mutations. The HOF would centralize all of these.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute handler. Use the rbac callback with assertWorkPackageProjectMembership from lib/auth/project.ts and a per-method bodySchema. Remove TODO.

title: Non-uniform error envelope
description: All error responses use { error: 'string' } instead of the project-standard ApiErrorBody envelope { success: false, error: { code, message, details } }. Frontend cannot reliably distinguish error codes.
suggested_fix: Throw new ApiError(status, code, message) from inside the handler; withRoute will format uniformly. For raw parts (still in this file), return the envelope shape.

title: RBAC duplicated — should use assertWorkPackageProjectMembership helper
description: Manual member.findUnique + role.permissions.includes('WORK_PACKAGE_EDIT'/'WORK_PACKAGE_DELETE') is duplicated from the Phase 7 RBAC layer. Easy to drift (e.g. permission string mismatch, missing isSystemAdmin bypass).
suggested_fix: Replace inline RBAC with await assertWorkPackageProjectMembership(workPackage.projectId, session.user.id, !!session.user.isSystemAdmin, 'WORK_PACKAGE_EDIT').

title: Update + activity create + notification create run outside a transaction
description: prisma.workPackage.update + prisma.activity.create + prisma.notification.create + emitActivity are 4 separate awaits. If the activity write fails after the WP update succeeds, the audit log drifts from the source-of-truth. Same for assignee notification create.
suggested_fix: Wrap update + activity.create in prisma.$transaction([...]). Keep notification + emitActivity + SSE outside the transaction (they have side effects), but ensure the activity row is durable inside.

title: Sentry.captureException never called
description: Mutating error handler only console.error's — no Sentry capture. Production debugging requires manual log scraping.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute so the HOF's catch block calls Sentry.captureException(err) when NODE_ENV=production.

title: Raw handler — no withRoute, no rate limit, no Sentry
description: /api/health is intentionally public but is not wrapped in withRoute({ public: true }). Inconsistent 405 envelope ({error} vs project envelope). Rate limiting absent — comment acknowledges DoS risk.
suggested_fix: Wrap with withRoute({ public: true, methods: ['GET'] }). Add an explicit rateLimitKey override that uses IP and a tight per-minute limit. Keep raw $queryRaw since it's the point of the probe.

title: Raw $queryRaw SQL in route
description: Health probe uses $queryRaw\`SELECT 1\`. With Prisma 7 this is generally safe (parameterless template) but bypasses any future migration safety net. Acceptable for a healthcheck.
suggested_fix: Acceptable as-is. Document explicitly that this is the only intentional $queryRaw in the API surface. Consider moving to prisma.$queryRawUnsafe('SELECT 1') with a TODO removed.

title: Raw handler — no withRoute, no envelope
description: Prometheus scrape endpoint returns plain text (correct) but error path returns res.status(500).send('Failed to collect metrics') which leaks internal details and doesn't follow the project envelope (though text/plain Prometheus output is fine). Method guard returns { error } not the envelope.
suggested_fix: Wrap with withRoute({ public: true, methods: ['GET'] }). For the body, keep res.send(metrics) — bypass envelope by returning a NextApiResponse-wrapped string from the handler (the HOF respects whatever the handler returns).

title: Raw handler — should use withRoute
description: Full NextApiHandler with manual session/rate-limit/method switch. Already had a P0 fix in Phase 7 (auth moved before method check) but is still not wrapped in withRoute. RBAC missing entirely on addMember (anyone authenticated can add members to any project — relying only on the URL having the project id). Inconsistent envelope (uses both {error: 'Unauthorized'} and {success: false, error: 'Unauthorized'}).
suggested_fix: Split into 4 routes (GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE) under withRoute, each with its own rbac callback (e.g. assertProjectMembership + PROJECT_MEMBER_MANAGE permission). Use ApiError everywhere.

title: Missing RBAC on POST (addMember)
description: addMember only checks session.user exists, NOT whether the caller is a project member with permission to add members. Any authenticated user can add any other user (and themselves, with any role) to any project.
suggested_fix: Use assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'MEMBER_CREATE') or equivalent permission.

title: PATCH updateMember has no auth/RBAC at all
description: updateMember calls zod parse then prisma.member.update directly without re-checking session.user. The top-level gate at line 30 is the only auth check, but no project-membership check is performed and no permission string is verified.
suggested_fix: After schema parse, call assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'MEMBER_UPDATE') and verify member.projectId === projectId (defense in depth).

title: Raw handler — but the real bug: no RBAC check
description: copyProject fetches the original project by ID and lets any authenticated user copy any project, including private ones. The top-level session check at line 20 is only auth, no project-membership or admin check. Also reads query.projectId (correct after Phase 7 fix) but the wrong-variable risk suggests further audit needed.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac callback: assertProjectMembership(originalProjectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'PROJECT_COPY'). Envelope: ApiError. The transaction itself is correct.

title: Race condition on identifier uniqueness check
description: The while-loop checks findUnique then increments, but two concurrent copies could both pick the same identifier. Should rely on DB unique constraint + retry on P2002, OR use findFirst with prefix and atomic counter.
suggested_fix: Let prisma.project.create throw on unique violation (P2002) and retry with counter++. Wrap the entire create in a try/catch that retries up to N times.

title: Member create + activity emit not transactional
description: prisma.member.create followed by emitActivity is two awaits. If emitActivity throws after the member is created, the join succeeds but the audit trail drifts.
suggested_fix: Wrap create + emitActivity (or the activity.create that emitActivity presumably performs) in a single prisma.$transaction. Or accept eventual consistency and document the trade-off.

title: POSITIVE — proper withRoute usage, GOOD reference
description: This file is a model citizen: withRoute HOF, zod body schema, ApiError throws, skipSentryFor for ZodError, transactional log+delete, system-admin gate before mutation. The other 95 raw handlers should follow this pattern.
suggested_fix: No fix needed. Use as a template for migrating the remaining raw handlers.

title: Raw handler — DELETE notification
description: DELETE-only handler with manual session + manual ownership check. Should be withRoute. Envelope is non-uniform ({error: 'string'} instead of ApiErrorBody). Missing rate limit and Sentry capture.
suggested_fix: Wrap with withRoute({ methods: ['DELETE'], rbac: async (session, { params }) => /* verify notification.userId === session.user.id */ }).

title: Raw handler — POST mark read
description: Same pattern as API-16. Ownership check is good (notification.userId === session.user.id via findFirst filter) but envelope is non-uniform.
suggested_fix: Wrap with withRoute({ methods: ['POST'] }).

title: Raw handler — POST read-all
description: Batch update of all unread notifications for current user. Same pattern. No body, so just methods/rbac needed.
suggested_fix: Wrap with withRoute({ methods: ['POST'] }).

title: Anti-pattern: swallow-and-return 200 OK on admin denial
description: Returns 200 with { sent: false, reason: 'insufficient_permissions' } for non-admin callers to prevent user enumeration. This is a security-vs-API-consistency trade-off, but it ALSO swallows server errors and zod validation failures as 200 OK. The comment says 'S1-F: 即使錯誤也返回 200' but this means errors are silent — bad data slips through, client can't react.
suggested_fix: Keep the 200-on-admin-deny behavior (it IS a security feature). For zod errors and other exceptions, return proper 400/500 with the envelope. Only the admin-deny path should return 200 OK with sent:false.

title: isSystemAdmin called instead of session.user.isSystemAdmin
description: Calls isSystemAdmin(session.user.id) which re-fetches the user. session.user.isSystemAdmin (already in JWT) avoids the DB hit. Inconsistent with other routes that use the JWT flag.
suggested_fix: Use session.user.isSystemAdmin from the JWT augmented Session. If the JWT is stale, the admin demotion will take effect within 1 hour (TTL).

title: Zod schema too permissive
description: userId: z.string() (no .cuid() or .min(1)), reason: z.string() (no enum, no length), resourceType: z.string() (no enum). Any string is accepted — could result in junk data.
suggested_fix: Tighten: userId: z.string().cuid(), reason: z.enum(['assigned','mentioned','watched','...']), resourceType: z.enum(['work_package','wiki','forum','...']).

title: Raw handler — 2FA disable bypasses withRoute
description: Sensitive mutation (disable 2FA) uses raw handler. Manual session + self-or-admin RBAC is correct logic but the envelope is wrong ({error: 'string'}). Missing Sentry capture, audit log (very important for security events), and rate limit. No try/catch around prisma.update — unhandled promise rejection on DB error.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute with bodySchema, methods: ['POST'], explicit auditLog callback (security event), skipSentryFor for ZodError. Add try/catch or rely on HOF error path.

title: 2FA disable has NO try/catch around DB updates
description: prisma.user.update (line 77) and prisma.webAuthnCredential.deleteMany (line 87) are called without try/catch. A DB error throws an unhandled rejection, leaking the actual error to the client and producing an ugly 500 with no envelope.
suggested_fix: Wrap both writes in a single prisma.$transaction. Then wrap in try/catch that returns ApiError(500, 'INTERNAL_ERROR').

title: Backup code lookup parses JSON inline + no encryption-at-rest note
description: JSON.parse(user.backupCodes) then iterates to find an unused code. If user.backupCodes is malformed (DB tampered), parse throws and the request 500s without envelope. Also no rate limiting on backup code attempts (brute force a 10-char code).
suggested_fix: Wrap parse in try/catch. Add a Redis-backed attempt counter per userId (5 attempts/15min) — re-use lib/ratelimit with a 2FA-specific key prefix.

title: Raw handler — 2FA status
description: GET handler with manual auth and self-or-admin check. Returns plaintext status fields (totpEnabled, webAuthnEnabled, backupCodesRemaining). No envelope. Missing rate limit, Sentry.
suggested_fix: Wrap with withRoute({ methods: ['GET'], rbac: (session, { params }) => session.user.id === params.id || session.user.isSystemAdmin }).

title: Raw handler — 2FA setup, exposes backup codes in response
description: Returns plain backup codes (line 72: backupCodes: codes) ONCE to user, plus hashedCodesJson. Correct UX but raw handler, no envelope, no rate limit (high-value attack target — could repeatedly trigger setup to cause email enumeration via WebAuthn challenge).
suggested_fix: Wrap with withRoute({ methods: ['POST'], bodySchema: setupSchema, rbac: self-or-admin }). Add rate-limit via rateLimitKey override keyed to userId+method.

title: Raw handler — 2FA verify, no rate limiting
description: verifyTOTP path is highly attackable (6-digit codes = 1M space). No rate limit on this endpoint. Brute force is trivial.
suggested_fix: Wrap with withRoute + custom rateLimitKey (userId+ip). Cap at 5 attempts per 15min via rateLimitKey returning a 2fa-specific bucket. Consider lockout after N failures.

title: TOTP setup is not transactional
description: Multiple writes: prisma.user.update for secondFactorPending is single write, but the Redis write for WebAuthn challenge is separate. If the DB write succeeds but the caller crashes before WebAuthn challenge is persisted (or vice-versa), state is inconsistent.
suggested_fix: For TOTP path: wrap in try/catch returning ApiError. For WebAuthn: order operations so Redis write happens first (the source-of-truth for the challenge) then DB update, with a finally that cleans Redis on failure.

title: Raw handler — query CRUD
description: GET/PATCH/DELETE in one handler with manual session check. PATCH isDefault logic uses updateMany to clear other defaults — not wrapped in transaction with the actual update. Envelope is {error: 'string'}.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute with bodySchema for PATCH, paramsSchema for [id], and methods split into 3 routes. Wrap the isDefault-clear + update in prisma.$transaction.

title: Uncaught throw in catch block
description: Inside the PATCH catch, the non-ZodError case calls `throw error` which propagates to the unhandled-rejection path. Returns a generic 500 with no envelope because there is no top-level try/catch.
suggested_fix: Wrap the whole handler in try/catch that maps the error to ApiError. Or migrate to withRoute so the HOF handles it.

title: Raw handler — saved-query list/create
description: Same as API-29. POST creates a savedQuery but if isDefault=true, the updateMany to clear other defaults runs OUTSIDE the create — race condition + audit drift.
suggested_fix: Wrap in prisma.$transaction([updateMany, create]). Or migrate to withRoute and add explicit transaction in handler body.

title: Raw handler — email send, admin-only RBAC
description: Manual RBAC: prisma.member.findFirst with role.name IN ('Admin','Project Manager'). This bypasses session.user.isSystemAdmin and the new assertProjectMembership helpers. Also: a project-level Admin or PM can send emails to ANY address (no project scope check on template data).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. RBAC: if template is project-scoped, require assertProjectMembership. For system templates (mention, meeting_invitation), require isSystemAdmin OR project membership of the project in data.projectId.

title: Zod schema for `to` accepts array of arbitrary strings
description: z.union([z.string(), z.array(z.string())]) — no email validation. `to: 'not-an-email'` would be accepted. SMTP providers will reject, but the queue table fills with junk.
suggested_fix: Use z.string().email() for single, z.array(z.string().email()).max(50) for arrays.

title: No transaction between send + queue-fallback
description: If sendEmail returns success=false, queueEmail is called without transaction. If queueEmail fails, the email is lost silently (console.warn only).
suggested_fix: If sendEmail fails AND queueEmail fails, throw ApiError(503, 'EMAIL_QUEUE_FAILED'). Surface the failure so caller can retry instead of dropping on the floor.

title: Raw handler — cron auth check is good but no rate limit
description: Uses CRON_SECRET Bearer token correctly. No withRoute wrapper. Defense-in-depth note: cron secrets in URLs / GitHub Actions logs can leak. Also no rate limit — a stolen secret could spam the endpoint.
suggested_fix: Wrap with withRoute({ public: true, methods: ['POST'], rbac: custom (verify CRON_SECRET) }). Add a rate-limit keyed to the secret itself (low limit, e.g. 10/min).

title: Plain string compare for CRON_SECRET — vulnerable to timing attack
description: provided !== expected compares two strings with short-circuit equality, which leaks timing information about the expected secret length and bytes.
suggested_fix: Use crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(provided), Buffer.from(expected)) after a length pre-check (also timing-safe).

title: Raw handler — sprint CRUD, missing RBAC
description: Manual session check, NO project-membership check. Any authenticated user can list sprints or create sprints on any project. POST body parsed with manual `if (!name...)` instead of zod. No try/catch around prisma.sprint.create.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Use assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'SPRINT_CREATE'). Use zod bodySchema. Wrap in try/catch or rely on HOF.

title: Raw handler — sprint detail/update/delete, missing RBAC
description: Same as API-37. PATCH/DELETE have no project-membership check. sprint.update/delete use { where: { id } } directly — an attacker who knows any sprintId could update or delete it (even cross-project).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute with rbac callback asserting project membership AND that the sprint belongs to the project.

title: Sprint DELETE without transaction with activity emit
description: prisma.sprint.delete is followed by no activity emit AND no transactional cleanup. Sprint may have child work-package references that fail the FK constraint and throw — no envelope returned.
suggested_fix: Either wrap delete + activity in transaction, OR add explicit handling of FK constraint errors with ApiError(409, 'SPRINT_HAS_DEPENDENCIES').

title: Raw handler — WIP limits, no RBAC
description: PATCH can change WIP limits for any project as long as the caller is authenticated. No project-membership check, no admin check. Also uses manual rate-limit + envelope is mixed ({success:false,error} sometimes, {error} other times).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'PROJECT_SETTINGS_EDIT').

title: Params from req.query.projectId — not validated
description: projectId taken from query string but not validated as cuid. Invalid values produce DB errors not handled by the route.
suggested_fix: Use withRoute paramsSchema: z.object({ projectId: z.string().cuid() }).

title: Raw handler — module toggle, wrong param name
description: Line 37: `const projectId = query.id as string` — but the folder is `[projectId]`. So query.id is undefined and line 39 returns 400. The handler is DEAD CODE for its intended purpose (Phase 7 Sprint A4 fix may have been partial).
suggested_fix: Fix to `query.projectId as string`, then migrate to withRoute with rbac assertProjectMembership(..., 'PROJECT_MODULE_EDIT').

title: N upserts in single $transaction — no error mapping
description: data.modules.map → prisma.projectModule.upsert inside $transaction. If any upsert fails (e.g. unique conflict not caught by upsert), the transaction rolls back but the error path returns {error: 'string'} not envelope.
suggested_fix: Wrap in try/catch that throws ApiError with the failed module info. Or migrate to withRoute.

title: Raw handler — groups list/create, admin gate via DB hit
description: Manual session + isSystemAdmin() DB call instead of session.user.isSystemAdmin JWT check. GET has NO admin gate — any authenticated user can list ALL groups + member counts (info disclosure).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. GET: require admin. POST: rbac callback using session.user.isSystemAdmin. Use bodySchema.

title: Raw handler — group detail/put/delete, GET has no admin gate
description: GET /api/groups/[id] leaks group membership (with user email + avatarUrl) to any authenticated user. PUT/DELETE correctly require isSystemAdmin() but use DB hit not JWT. DELETE has no audit log.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. GET: rbac requiring admin OR group membership. PUT/DELETE: rbac requiring admin (use session.user.isSystemAdmin, not DB hit).

title: Raw handler — group members CRUD
description: GET lists ALL members of ANY group (info disclosure). POST/DELETE require isSystemAdmin via DB hit. Inconsistent envelope. DELETE doesn't verify the userId belongs to the group before delete.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. GET: admin-only or group-member-only. Mutations: admin-only via JWT. paramsSchema: z.object({ id: z.string().cuid(), userId: z.string().cuid().optional() }) for DELETE.

title: isSystemAdmin gate but NO withRoute — race condition possible
description: GET handler is gated by isSystemAdmin() — but if the session JWT was issued before the user was demoted, the gate will pass for up to 1 hour. Also isSystemAdmin() does a DB call per request, slowing every branding GET.
suggested_fix: Use session.user.isSystemAdmin from JWT (already in token). Migrate to withRoute. Note: this is correct behavior because the JWT is the source-of-truth for short-lived admin checks; the DB call is over-engineering.

title: Zod regex for color too strict
description: primaryColor: z.string().regex(/^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$/) — only 6-digit hex. No support for 3-digit shorthand (#f00), 8-digit with alpha (#f00a), or named CSS colors.
suggested_fix: Accept either 3, 6, or 8 hex digits: z.string().regex(/^#([0-9A-Fa-f]{3}|[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}|[0-9A-Fa-f]{8})$/). Or use a permissive URL/color parser library.

title: Raw handler — file upload URL, missing RBAC helper
description: Manual project-membership check (prisma.member.findUnique) instead of assertProjectMembership helper. Inconsistent envelope (mixes successResponse/errorResponse with custom shape). No rate limit on URL generation — easy to enumerate storage keys.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac callback: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'FILE_UPLOAD'). Use bodySchema. Add rate-limit per-user.

title: Storage key creation not transactional with DB record
description: generateUploadUrl() (S3 side effect) and prisma.projectFile.create are sequential awaits. If the DB write fails after S3 URL mint, the URL is invalid for a non-existent file. Conversely, if S3 fails but DB succeeds, the row points to a key that was never pre-signed.
suggested_fix: Create DB row first with status='PENDING'. After successful S3 pre-sign, update row to status='READY'. Or wrap S3 call in try/catch and roll back DB on failure.

title: Filename sanitization strips unicode, could collide
description: filename.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]/g, '_') collapses Chinese/Japanese/etc. filenames into all underscores — different uploads of '测试.pdf' and '文档.pdf' both become '___.pdf'.
suggested_fix: Keep original filename in DB but encode for storage key: storageKey = `projects/${projectId}/files/${uuid}-${encodeURIComponent(originalFilename)}`. Validate storageKey with a stricter regex on the encoded form.

title: Raw handler — file metadata/DELETE, inconsistent RBAC
description: DELETE requires isUploader OR Admin role — but 'Admin' role-name string is hardcoded (line 37). If Phase 7 RBAC perm strings change, this silently degrades. GET has NO permission check — any authenticated user can read any file metadata (info disclosure).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. GET: rbac = assertProjectMembership(file.projectId, ..., 'FILE_READ'). DELETE: rbac = (file.uploadedById === session.user.id) || assertProjectMembership(..., 'FILE_DELETE_ADMIN').

title: Raw handler — file download URL
description: Manual membership check + uploader bypass. No rate limit (presigned URL mint can be abused). Inconsistent envelope shape.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: same as API-52 GET. Use withRoute's rate limit per user (presigned URL generation is an attack surface).

title: local-upload allows arbitrary path traversal in `key`
description: Authorization extracts projectId from `projects/<projectId>/...` then verifies membership, but then writes the file to whatever `key` was passed in. If key = `projects/PROJ_X/files/../../etc/passwd`, the path traversal is still possible because only the projectId prefix is matched, not the full normalized key.
suggested_fix: After membership check, normalize key with path.normalize and reject if it contains '..' OR doesn't start with `projects/<projectId>/` AND the user is not a project member. Also resolve key and verify the resolved path is within uploads/ directory.

title: Raw handler — file upload, inconsistent envelope
description: Returns {error: 'STRING_CODE'} not the project ApiErrorBody envelope. Manual session + membership check. bodyParser disabled (good for multipart) means withRoute's bodySchema wouldn't apply anyway — but the route can still use withRoute with bodySchema omitted + explicit multipart handling inside.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute({ methods: ['POST'] }) WITHOUT bodySchema (multipart). Inside the handler, do project-membership check via assertProjectMembership. Throw ApiError for every error. Wrap the whole pipeline in try/catch that returns ApiError(500, 'UPLOAD_FAILED').

title: No DB record written after successful upload
description: After streaming the file to disk, no prisma.projectFile.create call. Uploaded files are orphaned — search, audit, and download flows can't find them. (S3 upload-url.ts DOES write the record, but local-upload.ts doesn't.)
suggested_fix: After successful pipeline, call prisma.projectFile.create with same shape as upload-url.ts. Wrap pipeline + DB write in transaction if possible.

title: Path traversal in `key` param — accepted from query string
description: req.query.key is the storageKey (filesystem path component). Attacker controls it. While the projectId prefix is checked, the remainder of the path is unvalidated.
suggested_fix: Use paramsSchema: z.object({ key: z.string().regex(/^projects/[^/]+/files/[^/]+$/) }). Reject any key containing '..' or absolute paths.

title: Raw handler — LDAP servers, no zod, no RBAC layer
description: Manual session.user.isSystemAdmin check (correct for LDAP — only system admins should configure LDAP), but no zod validation of body. createServer accepts arbitrary req.body fields including port (string coercion issues), useSsl (no type check), bindPassword (plaintext stored). No envelope. No try/catch around prisma.ldapServer.create.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute with bodySchema using zod for all fields. Use rbac: (session) => session.user.isSystemAdmin === true. Throw ApiError for all error paths.

title: bindPassword stored plaintext — security risk
description: bindPassword stored as plaintext in prisma.ldapServer.bindPassword. Anyone with DB read access (or a SQL injection bug elsewhere) gets all LDAP bind credentials.
suggested_fix: Encrypt with libsodium or KMS before storing. Decrypt in lib/ldap/client before use. Add a key rotation flow.

title: Raw handler — single LDAP server CRUD
description: Same pattern as API-58. PUT updates fields without zod. POST /test endpoint runs testConnection() against the LDAP server — unbounded, no timeout, could DoS the admin endpoint.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute with split routes per method. Add 30s timeout on testConnection. Use zod bodySchema for PUT.

title: Raw handler — LDAP sync, accepts plaintext password in body
description: POST /api/ldap/sync takes username+password in body and calls syncLdapUser. The password is the user's actual LDAP password used for the bind. Logging systems (Vercel logs, Sentry) may capture the request body and leak credentials.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Add skipSentryFor and auditLog that excludes body from log. Consider token-based sync (short-lived) instead of password. NEVER log the body of sync requests.

title: GET method for a write operation (full sync)
description: GET /api/ldap/sync triggers fullSync() — a destructive write (modifies user records). REST convention is POST for mutations. GET should be idempotent and cacheable; this is neither.
suggested_fix: Change to POST. Update frontend calls. If you keep GET, add explicit no-cache headers and document the side effect.

title: Raw handler — LDAP mappings, no zod
description: Manual field checks instead of zod. No RBAC layer. No envelope. createMapping has no check for duplicate ldapGroupDn + ldapServerId — DB unique constraint will throw but with no envelope.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. zod schema: z.object({ ldapServerId: z.string().cuid(), ldapGroupDn: z.string().min(1).max(512), ldapGroupName: z.string().min(1).max(255), localRoleId: z.string().cuid() }). Handle P2002 → ApiError(409, 'DUPLICATE_MAPPING').

title: Full sync via GET has no rate limit
description: GET /api/ldap/sync runs fullSync — expensive operation that pulls all LDAP users + groups. No rate limit. Attacker who steals admin JWT could DoS by spamming it.
suggested_fix: Add per-user rate limit (1 sync/5min). Convert to POST. withRoute's rateLimitKey can return a sync-specific bucket.

title: Raw handler — webhooks list/create
description: Correct admin-only auth (Phase 6 fix), but still raw handler. Inconsistent envelope shape (sometimes {error: 'string'}, sometimes {error: zod.errors}). No rate limit on webhook creation (could create thousands).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: (session) => session.user.isSystemAdmin === true. bodySchema. Tighten events validation to z.array(z.enum(WEBHOOK_EVENTS)).

title: Event validation runs AFTER zod parse
description: isValidWebhookEvent check is a separate manual loop after zod. If the zod schema were z.array(z.enum(WEBHOOK_EVENTS as [string, ...string[]]), this validation step would be unnecessary.
suggested_fix: Use zod enum: events: z.array(z.enum(WEBHOOK_EVENTS as [string, ...string[]])).min(1). Remove manual isValidWebhookEvent loop.

title: Inconsistent zod error response shape
description: Returns res.status(400).json({ error: error.errors }) which is zod's raw error array (array of objects), not the standard envelope.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute; the HOF's formatErrorBody handles ZodError → { code: 'VALIDATION_ERROR', message: '...', details: err.issues }.

title: Raw handler — webhook detail/put/delete, GET has NO admin gate
description: Auth gate at line 19 just checks session.user exists. NO isSystemAdmin check on GET. Any authenticated user can read any webhook's URL + secret + recent deliveries (massive data leak — webhook URLs often contain internal endpoints).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: (session) => session.user.isSystemAdmin === true for ALL methods (not just mutations).

title: GET /api/webhooks/[id] returns webhook secret
description: getWebhook returns prisma.webhook.findUnique with full record including secret. If the secret is used for HMAC signing, exposing it lets attackers forge webhooks.
suggested_fix: Strip secret from the response (similar to LDAP bindPassword). Only return secret in a separate admin-only 'reveal' endpoint with audit log.

title: Raw handler — deliveries, only auth check, no admin gate
description: Same as API-68: only session.user exists check, no admin. Any authenticated user can read delivery history (including payloads that may contain user data).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: (session, { params }) => session.user.isSystemAdmin. Add querySchema for limit/offset/status validation.

title: POSITIVE — proper withRoute usage, GOOD reference
description: This file is a model citizen: withRoute HOF, zod body schema with cuid array validation, ApiError throws, per-project transactions for isolation, skipSentryFor for ZodError, system-admin gate. The other 95 raw handlers should follow this pattern.
suggested_fix: No fix needed. Use as a template for migrating the remaining raw handlers.

title: Uses string Error messages as error codes
description: throw new Error('PROJECT_NOT_FOUND') / throw new Error('ALREADY_ARCHIVED') — string matching in catch is brittle. If a future maintainer renames a message, the catch logic silently miscategorizes.
suggested_fix: Throw ApiError(404, 'PROJECT_NOT_FOUND', ...) / ApiError(409, 'ALREADY_ARCHIVED', ...) inside the transaction. Or define error constants. Or use custom Error subclasses.

title: Raw handler — time entries list/create
description: Method switch on line 17 happens BEFORE session check — anon GET on the switch would call getTimeEntries which checks session inside, but POST doesn't gate session until inside createTimeEntry. Manual session check + manual envelope (successResponse/errorResponse custom shape, NOT the project ApiErrorBody).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. withRoute handles auth at the top of the pipeline. Use bodySchema for POST, querySchema for GET filters (coerce strings/numbers/booleans properly).

title: GET has no project-membership filter — info disclosure
description: getTimeEntries accepts userId/projectId/workPackageId filters but doesn't verify the caller has permission to see the requested entries. Any authenticated user can pass ?userId=X and see any user's time entries (work hours + approver info — sensitive).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac callback: if userId filter is provided and !== session.user.id, require isSystemAdmin OR project membership of the projectId filter's project.

title: Date range filter not validated
description: from/to query params passed straight to new Date() without validation. Invalid strings produce Invalid Date which Prisma may handle differently from expected. No zod parse.
suggested_fix: Use withRoute querySchema: z.object({ from: z.string().date().optional(), to: z.string().date().optional(), userId: z.string().cuid().optional(), ... }).

title: GET time entry — no ownership check after auth
description: getTimeEntry (line 35) authenticates then loads any time entry by ID. No verification that entry.userId === session.user.id OR isSystemAdmin OR project membership. Any authenticated user can read any time entry by guessing/iterating IDs.
suggested_fix: After fetch: throw ApiError(403, 'FORBIDDEN', ...) unless entry.userId === session.user.id OR session.user.isSystemAdmin OR assertProjectMembership(entry.workPackage.projectId, session.user.id, ...).

title: Raw handler — time entry CRUD
description: Same auth/session pattern duplicated across 3 handlers (GET/PATCH/DELETE). Inconsistent envelope (uses custom successResponse/errorResponse, not ApiErrorBody).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Split into separate routes per method OR keep one withRoute and dispatch by req.method inside the handler.

title: Anyone authenticated can approve any time entry
description: Comment on line 27 admits: 'For now, we'll allow any logged-in user to approve (should be restricted by project membership)'. This is a CRITICAL RBAC gap. Any logged-in user can approve any submitted time entry, regardless of role or project.
suggested_fix: Add RBAC: assertProjectMembership(workPackage.projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'TIME_ENTRY_APPROVE'). Throw ApiError(403) if not allowed. Add audit log on approval.

title: Raw handler — approve, no envelope, no audit
description: No withRoute, no proper ApiError envelope, no audit log on approval (a sensitive financial action). No rate limit (could spam approvals to flood DB).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Add auditLog callback (security event). Add rateLimitKey override for approve.

title: Likely same RBAC gap as approve.ts
description: Based on the parallel structure with approve.ts, this file likely has the same 'anyone can reject' RBAC gap. Without reading the full file I cannot confirm but the pattern is identical to approve.
suggested_fix: Verify and apply same fix as API-78. Add assertProjectMembership with TIME_ENTRY_APPROVE permission.

title: Raw handler — submit, no rate limit
description: POST submit time entry. No rate limit (could spam status transitions). No envelope. No withRoute.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute with methods: ['POST']. Rate limit per user (10/min).

title: RBAC: ownership check inline, not via helper
description: existing.userId !== session.user.id is correct but should use a helper for consistency with the rest of the Phase 7 RBAC layer. Could be wrapped as isTimeEntryOwner(entry, session).
suggested_fix: Extract a small helper in lib/auth/project.ts: isTimeEntryOwner(timeEntry, sessionUser). Use it here and in submit.ts.

title: Raw handler — news CRUD, no zod, no project-membership
description: Manual auth + ownership-based RBAC. PATCH accepts raw req.body (no zod). DELETE uses isSystemAdmin() DB call instead of JWT. No project-membership check on PATCH (only ownership). Inconsistent envelope.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. PATCH bodySchema: z.object({title,summary,content} all optional). rbac: author OR admin via JWT.

title: PATCH without project-membership check
description: PATCH allows update if authorId === session.user.id only. Project members with edit-news permission cannot update others' news. Could be intentional or a gap depending on spec.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac callback: if (existing.authorId === session.user.id) return true; else return assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'NEWS_EDIT') verdict.

title: Raw handler — news comments, no project-membership check
description: Manual auth. POST adds a comment without verifying the caller is a project member of the project's news. Any logged-in user could comment on any project's news if they know the slug.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'NEWS_COMMENT').

title: Comment create + activity emit not transactional
description: Same pattern: prisma.newsComment.create followed by emitActivity without transaction. If emitActivity throws, comment exists without activity record.
suggested_fix: Move activity emit into a transaction with the create.

title: Raw handler — news list/create, race condition on slug
description: Manual auth. POST has the same while-loop slug uniqueness check as copy.ts — race condition possible. No zod bodySchema, no project-membership RBAC.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Use prisma.news.create and catch P2002 (unique violation), retry up to 5 times with counter++. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, ..., 'NEWS_CREATE').

title: News create + activity emit not transactional
description: Same audit-drift risk as API-86.
suggested_fix: Move activity emit into transaction.

title: Raw handler — activity feed, project-membership check inline
description: Manual prisma.member.findUnique check (correct) but inline. Complex response shape (manual activity grouping algorithm in handler) makes migration tricky. No zod querySchema for page/limit/filter.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'ACTIVITY_READ'). querySchema: z.object({ page: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).default(1), limit: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(50), filter: z.string().optional(), includeArchived: z.coerce.boolean().default(false) }).

title: VALID_SUBJECT_TYPES enum not derived from prisma
description: Hand-maintained array of subject types instead of using a shared constant or zod enum. Prisma schema is the source of truth for valid values — drift possible.
suggested_fix: Move to a shared types file (e.g. lib/types/activity.ts) as a const + z.enum, and re-export. Use the same constant in emitActivity.

title: Raw handler — budget list/create, no RBAC
description: Manual auth + zod. POST has NO project-membership check. Any logged-in user can create a budget for any project. GET has no membership check (info disclosure of project budget amounts).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'BUDGET_READ' / 'BUDGET_CREATE').

title: Raw handler — budget lines, no RBAC
description: Same as API-91. POST adds a budget line for any project. unitCost/quantity are user-controlled — no validation against existing budget cap.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'BUDGET_EDIT'). Add business rule: reject if sum of line.totalCost would exceed budget.amount.

title: Raw handler — budget report, no project-membership check
description: Manual auth only. GET budget report (financial data!) without verifying project membership. Any authenticated user can read budget variance for any project by guessing budget id.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'BUDGET_REPORT_VIEW').

title: `any` cast in reduce — type safety hole
description: Line 25: `(te as any).activity?.hourlyRate` casts the entire entry to any. If the activity model changes shape (rename or removal), this silently produces undefined.
suggested_fix: Use proper Prisma types. If activity.hourlyRate doesn't exist on the type, fix the schema/query — don't use `any` to paper over it.

title: Raw handler — project time-entries, no RBAC on GET
description: Manual auth + zod for POST. GET has NO project-membership check — any authenticated user can list all time entries for any project by passing the projectId in the URL. Same info disclosure as API-74.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'TIME_ENTRY_READ').

title: DELETE — anyone can soft-delete others' entries
description: DELETE accepts comma-separated IDs and soft-deletes them. No ownership check on each entry. Any authenticated user who passes a list of valid entry IDs can mark them deleted (data integrity loss).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Inside handler, for each id verify entry.userId === session.user.id OR assertProjectMembership(...,'TIME_ENTRY_DELETE'). Skip (don't delete) unauthorized entries. Or reject the whole request if any unauthorized.

title: Soft delete + N activity emits not transactional
description: prisma.timeEntry.updateMany + for-loop of emitActivity — N sequential awaits. If one activity emit fails, entries are already deleted but only some activity rows written.
suggested_fix: Wrap updateMany + a single bulk emitActivity call in transaction. Or use prisma.activity.createMany for batch emit.

title: Time entry create + activity emit not transactional
description: Same audit-drift pattern as before.
suggested_fix: Wrap create + emit in transaction.

title: Raw handler — documents, project-membership check inline
description: Manual prisma.member.findUnique — should use assertProjectMembership helper. Inconsistent envelope (mixes {error: 'UNAUTHORIZED'} and {error: 'FORBIDDEN'} and {error: 'VALIDATION_ERROR', details}). POST has no body validation beyond the safeParse. No try/catch around prisma.document.create — DB error leaks raw. POST create + activity emit not transactional.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'DOCUMENT_CREATE'). Use withRoute's bodySchema + Sentry capture.

title: Raw handler — document detail/update/delete
description: Same membership-check-inline pattern. PATCH has no permission check beyond membership (any project member can edit any document). DELETE has no audit beyond emitActivity (no project-specific audit log).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertDocumentProjectMembership(projectId, documentId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'DOCUMENT_EDIT'/'DOCUMENT_DELETE').

title: Soft delete + activity emit not transactional
description: prisma.document.update (soft delete) followed by emitActivity. Audit-drift risk.
suggested_fix: Wrap in transaction.

title: Raw handler — document versions, no project-membership check
description: Manual auth. NO project-membership check before listing/creating versions of a document. Any authenticated user can read or create versions for any document by passing the id.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertDocumentProjectMembership(projectId, id, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'DOCUMENT_VERSION_CREATE').

title: Version create + activity emit not transactional
description: Same audit-drift risk.
suggested_fix: Wrap in transaction. Also bump document.version in the same transaction.

title: Raw handler — folders, project-membership inline
description: Manual membership check. POST has no further permission (any project member can create folders — may be intentional). No try/catch.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'FOLDER_CREATE').

title: Soft delete returns hard-coded deletedAt date
description: Line 130: `return res.status(200).json({ deletedAt: new Date() })` returns a date string from a fresh new Date() call, not the actual deletedAt column value. The client may see a different timestamp than the DB.
suggested_fix: Return the actual deletedAt from prisma.document.update. Or just return { deleted: true }.

title: Raw handler — folder detail/update/delete
description: Manual membership check. PATCH has no permission check (any project member can rename). DELETE has no permission check.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertFolderProjectMembership(projectId, id, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'FOLDER_EDIT'/'FOLDER_DELETE').

title: DELETE folder not transactional with content check
description: Check folder is empty, then delete. Race condition: between the check and delete, another request could add a document. Use a transaction with re-check.
suggested_fix: Wrap findFirst+_count+delete in a transaction. Re-check counts inside the tx.

title: Raw handler — wiki comments, no project-membership check
description: Manual auth. POST adds a comment without verifying project membership. Same pattern as news comments.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertWikiPageBySlugProjectMembership(projectId, slug, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'WIKI_COMMENT').

title: Activity + comment create not transactional
description: Two sequential creates. If the second fails, an activity row exists with no comment attached.
suggested_fix: Wrap prisma.activity.create + prisma.activityComment.create in a single transaction. Or migrate to a single prisma call with nested write.

title: Raw handler — wiki restore, no project-membership check
description: Manual auth. POST restores a wiki version without verifying project membership OR that the user has WIKI_EDIT permission. Sensitive (overwrites current content).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertWikiPageBySlugProjectMembership(projectId, slug, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'WIKI_RESTORE_VERSION').

title: Restore transaction is good but activity emit is outside
description: The version-create + page-update is transactional (good). But the emitActivity call is outside the transaction. If emit fails, the restore happens without audit.
suggested_fix: Move emitActivity into the transaction, OR add the activity create as part of the tx and use a separate emit for SSE.

title: Raw handler — repository list/create, NO RBAC
description: Manual auth + name check. GET lists ALL repositories for a project (info disclosure). POST creates a repository with no project-membership check — any authenticated user can attach any repository URL to any project.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'REPOSITORY_READ'/'REPOSITORY_CREATE'). Validate URL with zod (must be git URL, no file:// schemes).

title: POST accepts arbitrary repository URL with no validation
description: url: z.string() (implicit, no validation) — accepts file://, ssh:// with arbitrary host, javascript:, etc. Could point at internal infra or exfiltrate data.
suggested_fix: Use zod: url: z.string().url().refine(u => /^https?:///.test(u) || /^git@/.test(u)).

title: POST body parsed manually, not with zod
description: `if (!name)` is the only validation. type/url/localPath not validated at all.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute with bodySchema: z.object({ name: z.string().min(1).max(255), type: z.enum(['GIT','SVN','HG']).default('GIT'), url: z.string().url().optional(), localPath: z.string().regex(/^/[a-zA-Z0-9._/-]+$/).optional() }).

title: Raw handler — burndown, NO RBAC
description: Manual auth + 0 RBAC. GET returns burndown data for any sprint. POST writes burndown data with no validation (date/remaining from raw req.body).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: assertProjectMembership(projectId, session.user.id, isSystemAdmin, 'SPRINT_BURNDOWN_READ'/'SPRINT_BURNDOWN_WRITE'). bodySchema for POST.

title: POSITIVE — proper withRoute usage, GOOD reference
description: This file is a model citizen: withRoute HOF, ApiError throws, skipSentryFor for ZodError, returns spec-prescribed shape with 200 + null. The other 95 raw handlers should follow this pattern.
suggested_fix: No fix needed.

title: POSITIVE — proper withRoute usage, GOOD reference
description: withRoute HOF, bodySchema, checkProjectPermission RBAC, idempotent invite check (409 on duplicate), best-effort email, secure token generation. Model route.
suggested_fix: No fix needed.

title: POSITIVE — proper withRoute usage, GOOD reference
description: withRoute HOF, checkProjectPermission RBAC, clean spec-prescribed response shape.
suggested_fix: No fix needed.

title: Raw handler — API key management, no envelope, no rate limit
description: Sensitive endpoint (creates API keys for the whole user account). Uses v3Error formatter (OpenProject v3 API compatibility) instead of project envelope. No rate limit (attacker could trigger re-key generation). POST overwrites any existing key without notice (line 87).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Add auditLog (security event). Rate-limit per user. In POST, notify user via email when key changes.

title: API key returned plaintext once and lost forever
description: POST returns apiKey once. If the user copies it, the plaintext key is in the response body forever (browser cache, devtools, server logs). Recommend a separate 'reveal' flow that requires re-authentication.
suggested_fix: Add a separate POST /api/api-key/reveal endpoint with re-auth (password + TOTP) that returns the key only after explicit confirmation.

title: Raw handler — 2FA verify at login, NO rate limit, NO zod
description: CRITICAL security endpoint with NO rate limit. Brute force TOTP (1M space) is trivial. NO zod validation — token could be any string. Backup code path is especially weak (verifyBackupCode with a single-element array is essentially a string compare).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Add Redis-backed rate limit per user (5 attempts/15min). zod bodySchema. After 5 failures, lock account until TOTP re-setup.

title: Backup code verification — hashedCodes vs plaintext logic flaw
description: Line 39: `verifyBackupCode(backupCode, [h])` — passes only the single hash to verify, but verifyBackupCode likely iterates the array. Line 41 replaces the matched hash with 'USED' literal string. If the column is typed as string[], then storing 'USED' literal works. But if it's compared as a hash, the next verify will treat 'USED' as a valid input format and re-hash it, possibly matching against future inputs. Need to verify verifyBackupCode handles 'USED' gracefully.
suggested_fix: Audit verifyBackupCode in lib/2fa/backup-codes.ts. Ensure 'USED' marker cannot be matched by future verifies. Consider using a separate `used: boolean[]` array instead of overwriting the hash.

title: Raw handler — 2FA setup, no zod, no rate limit
description: Multi-action endpoint (generate/enable/disable) controlled by `action` field — no zod enum validation. attacker can pass any action. Disable path (line 49) has NO re-auth — anyone with a stolen session can disable 2FA.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Split into 3 separate routes (POST /api/auth/2fa/setup/generate, /enable, /disable). For /disable, require password re-confirmation in body.

title: Disable 2FA without re-authentication
description: action='disable' just clears totpEnabled and secret. No password check, no TOTP confirmation. A stolen session cookie = 2FA disabled.
suggested_fix: Disable must require password + TOTP in body, just like users/[id]/2fa/disable.ts. Or migrate to withRoute and add an explicit re-auth check.

title: Raw handler — LDAP auth, plaintext password in body
description: LDAP authentication with username+password in body. Same body-logging risks as sync.ts. No envelope. No rate limit (brute force).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute({ public: true, methods: ['POST'] }). Body schema with rate limit per IP+username. Configure skipSentryFor + auditLog that excludes body.

title: Error message leaked from sync
description: syncResult.error returned directly to client. May reveal whether user exists, server reachable, etc.
suggested_fix: Return generic 'Authentication failed' regardless of specific error. Log the specific error server-side.

title: `syncResult.user!` non-null assertion
description: Line 54: `syncResult.user!.id` — non-null assertion without runtime check. If syncResult.success is true but user is undefined, this throws TypeError. Also the `if (!user)` check on line 64 only fires for the DB lookup; the assertion on line 54 is unguarded.
suggested_fix: Replace `!` with explicit check: if (!syncResult.user) throw new Error('User not synced') or return 500.

title: CSP report endpoint — no withRoute (acceptable as public)
description: CSP violation reports are public POST endpoints that browsers send. withRoute({ public: true }) would be cleaner but the current implementation is OK. No auth/rate limit is intentional (CSP reports don't carry session).
suggested_fix: Wrap with withRoute({ public: true, methods: ['POST'] }) — keeps the pipeline (logging, rate-limit, audit) consistent. Use a dedicated high-limit rateLimitKey to avoid DOS.

title: Raw handler — custom fields, no zod, manual admin gate
description: Manual isSystemAdmin() DB call. POST body validated manually with `if (!name)` checks + string-includes for enum. No try/catch around prisma.customField.create.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: (session) => session.user.isSystemAdmin === true. bodySchema using z.enum for fieldFormat + entityType.

title: Raw handler — custom field detail, isSystemAdmin via DB
description: Same as API-137. PUT manual field-by-field update with no zod. DELETE has no audit log.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac admin-only. bodySchema for PUT. Audit log on DELETE.

title: Raw handler — time-entries export, NO RBAC, info disclosure
description: Manual auth only. Any authenticated user can pass projectId or userId and get time entries for any project/user. CSV/PDF export of hours worked — sensitive HR data.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: if userId !== session.user.id, require isSystemAdmin OR assertProjectMembership(projectId, ...). querySchema for from/to/format.

title: PDF format branch returns same shape as JSON
description: Line 48 `if (format === 'pdf')` doesn't actually generate a PDF — it returns the same JSON shape with a 'summary' block. Misleading API contract.
suggested_fix: Either implement actual PDF generation (using pdfkit or similar) OR remove the format query parameter and only support JSON.

title: Date parsing without validation
description: from/to passed to new Date() without validation — Invalid Date produces unexpected Prisma queries. No zod parse.
suggested_fix: Use withRoute querySchema with z.coerce.date().

title: Raw handler — wiki export, isSystemAdmin via DB call
description: Manual auth + manual isSystemAdmin check via DB lookup. Manual membership check. No withRoute. Mixed envelope (successResponse vs raw {error: 'string'}).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Use session.user.isSystemAdmin (JWT) instead of DB hit. rbac: assertWikiPageBySlugProjectMembership OR admin.

title: isAdmin check uses full user fetch (overkill)
description: Lines 35-38: full prisma.user.findUnique just to read isSystemAdmin. JWT already has this. Same anti-pattern as several other routes.
suggested_fix: Use session.user.isSystemAdmin from the augmented Session. Document this pattern in CONTRIBUTING.md.

title: Raw handler — local download, inconsistent envelope + path traversal
description: Same as local-upload.ts: path-traversal risk if storageKey has '..'. Inconsistent {error: 'STRING_CODE'} envelope. Manual membership + uploader check.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Validate key with regex (no '..', no absolute paths). Use ApiError envelope. Use assertProjectMembership helper.

title: Content-Disposition filename from user-controlled path
description: `filename="${key.split('/').pop()}"` uses the storageKey's last segment as the download filename. If storageKey contains CRLF or quotes, HTTP header injection. Also Content-Type is hardcoded `application/octet-stream` — should use the stored contentType from ProjectFile.
suggested_fix:
Look up the ProjectFile record (already in DB, line 53) and use its filename + contentType. Sanitize: replace any ["
] with '_' before embedding in the header.

title: Raw handler — LDAP sync trigger, `any` catch
description: Minimal handler with `e: any` catch. No envelope. No paramsSchema for [id]. No try/catch around the DB lookup that could fail before syncLdapToLocal. Returns error.message directly to client.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. paramsSchema: z.object({ id: z.string().cuid() }). Use ApiError for the 404 path. Don't leak error.message — map to ApiError(500, 'LDAP_SYNC_FAILED').

title: Raw handler — LDAP test connection
description: Manual isSystemAdmin + raw prisma lookup. No envelope. `e: any` catch leaks error.message. LDAP bind password read from DB and used without decryption (matches API-59 finding).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Decrypt bindPassword before use. Throw ApiError on bind failure. Don't leak error.message.

title: No timeout on LDAP bind
description: `bindClient(client, ...)` could hang indefinitely if the LDAP server is unreachable or in a firewall-blackhole. Browser request times out, server thread is held.
suggested_fix: Wrap bindClient in Promise.race with a 10s timeout. Or configure LDAP client timeout in lib/ldap/client.ts.

title: Raw handler — LDAP mapping detail/update/delete
description: Manual admin gate. PUT parses req.body with destructuring (no zod — accepts any string for ldapGroupDn/localRoleId). DELETE no audit log. No envelope.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. rbac: admin-only via JWT. bodySchema for PUT. Audit log on DELETE.

title: Raw handler — my-page layout, no withRoute
description: Manual session check + zod. GET is fine (read your own data). PUT upsert is fine (write your own data). No envelope issues. No rate limit.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Use bodySchema for PUT. Consider rate limit on PUT (low, 30/min) to prevent layout thrashing.

title: Double `as unknown as` cast — type safety hole
description: Lines 81 & 84: `parsed.data.widgets as unknown as object` — double cast indicates the Widget[] type doesn't match MyPageLayout.widgets (Prisma Json type). Should define a proper Prisma input type.
suggested_fix: Use Prisma.InputJsonValue type for the widgets field. Or use zod's transform to coerce to JSON-compatible shape.

title: Raw handler — notification settings
description: Manual session check. GET/PATCH work but no withRoute. notificationType: z.string() too permissive (no enum).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Tighten notificationType: z.enum(['assigned','mentioned','watched','project_updated','work_package_created','comment_replied','meeting_scheduled','...']).

title: Raw handler — unread count, but logic is correct
description: Manual session check. Cache layer is well-designed (graceful degradation on Redis error). GET-only. No withRoute.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. The cached helper inside is solid — keep it.

title: setCachedUnreadCount awaited in request path
description: Line 52: awaited `await setCachedUnreadCount(...)` — adds Redis round-trip to every cache miss. Could be fire-and-forget.
suggested_fix: Wrap in `void setCachedUnreadCount(...)` (don't await) — Redis write failures don't affect correctness anyway.
