suggested_fix: No fix needed.

title: POSITIVE — proper withRoute usage with public:true
description: withRoute HOF with public:true (token-as-credential pattern), zod bodySchema with strong password refinement, ApiError for all branches (NOT_FOUND, ALREADY_ACCEPTED, EXPIRED, NEW_USER_REQUIRES_CREDENTIALS), idempotent member creation.
suggested_fix: No fix needed.

title: Raw handler — global search, no withRoute, no project-scoping
description: Manual auth + zod. Searches wiki/forum/document/meeting/work_package across ALL projects for an authenticated user. Even with projectId filter, the user may not be a member of that project — they get results anyway (info disclosure). No withRoute. No rate limit (search is expensive — could DoS).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Add project-membership check when projectId is provided. Add per-user rate limit (30/min). For global searches, only return results from projects where the user is a member.

title: Search across all projects for global queries
description: When no projectId is provided, the search runs across all projects. Even after auth was added (Phase 4 Sprint 5), there is no per-project authorization filter. Members of project A could see results from project B.
suggested_fix: For global searches, add a `where: { project: { members: { some: { userId: session.user.id } } } }` filter to each search query. Or scope by accessibleProjectIds from assertProjectMembership helper.

title: Raw handler — SSE stream, manual auth, `(session.user as any)`
description: Uses `(session.user as any).id || (session.user as any).sub` — `any` cast twice. The augmented Session from next-auth should have a typed .id field. No withRoute (SSE streams are hard to wrap in HOF — but could use withRoute for the initial auth check then return the SSE handler).
suggested_fix: Use Session.user.id (typed) instead of any cast. Wrap the initial auth in withRoute({ public: false, methods: ['GET'] }) by returning a custom response handler from the HOF. Or keep raw but fix the any casts.

title: Graceful degradation leaks env state
description: Returns `{type:'unavailable', reason:'redis_not_configured'}` 200 OK to the client. The `reason` field exposes internal infra state to any client (info disclosure — client now knows Redis isn't configured for this deployment).
suggested_fix: Just return an empty SSE stream that closes. Or return `{type:'unavailable'}` without the reason field.

title: Raw handler — time report, isAdmin via DB call
description: Same pattern as API-143: manual isAdmin via full user.findUnique. Manual membership check. Manual envelope. No withRoute.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Use session.user.isSystemAdmin from JWT. Use assertProjectMembership helper. querySchema for projectId/userId/from/to/includeDeleted.

title: Authorization only when projectId provided
description: The membership check is wrapped in `if (projectId && typeof projectId === 'string')` — if projectId is missing or not a string, no authorization at all! Then the query filters by `where.workPackage = { projectId: undefined }` which would match all entries.
suggested_fix: If no projectId provided, return 400 BAD_REQUEST. Or require at least one of projectId/userId + assertProjectMembership for projectId.

title: Public API info endpoint, no withRoute (acceptable)
description: Pure static metadata endpoint. No auth needed (it's a version ping). Raw handler is fine but should still use withRoute({ public: true }) for consistency.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute({ public: true, methods: ['GET'] }) for consistency. No security impact.

title: Raw handler — v3 projects list, no withRoute
description: Manual session + API key auth. v3 API contract — uses v3 response formatters (deliberate, not the project envelope). No rate limit. Returns ALL projects with members (email + role) to any authenticated user OR anyone with a valid API key — no project-membership scoping.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute with a custom response wrapper that preserves v3 shape (use skipFormatBody or similar). Or keep raw but add: per-user rate limit; project-membership filter (only return projects where viewer is a member, unless admin).

title: offset/pageSize parsed manually without zod
description: No zod querySchema. Negative numbers or non-numeric strings produce NaN behavior in Math.max/min. No upper bound on offset (could be huge — slow query).
suggested_fix: Use withRoute querySchema: z.object({ offset: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).default(1), pageSize: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(20) }).

title: v3 work-packages — filter JSON parsed and used in Prisma where
description: parseFilters parses JSON from query string, then directly assigns user-controlled values to Prisma `where` object via `where[field] = cond.values[0]`. An attacker can inject `where['projectId'] = { in: [list of any project IDs] }` to read work packages from any project. The case '=' maps single values; case '~' uses contains+insensitive. No validation of field names against an allowlist.
suggested_fix: Use an allowlist of filterable fields (e.g. ['status','assigneeId','projectId','typeId','priorityId']). Validate field name with zod enum. Reject any unknown field.

title: Raw handler — v3 work-packages, no project-scoping on result
description: Even with valid filters, the where clause has no project membership scoping. Any authenticated user OR API key holder can list work packages across ALL projects.
suggested_fix: Add `where.OR = [{ project: { members: { some: { userId: viewerId } } } }, ...]` for non-admin viewers. Or for API key auth, use the API key's allowed-projects list.

title: Empty catch on JSON parse — invalid filter silently ignored
description: Line 48: catch {} swallows JSON.parse errors. A malformed filter just returns all work packages (the empty where object), which is broader than expected.
suggested_fix: Throw 400 on parse failure. Or treat malformed filters as no filters but log a warning.

title: Raw handler — v3 users list, admin-only via JWT
description: Manual session + JWT-based isSystemAdmin check (correct pattern). No withRoute. v3 envelope is intentional. No rate limit on PII dump endpoint.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Add per-user rate limit (10/min) on admin-only PII endpoints. Audit log on every call.

title: Raw handler — v3 user detail, passwordMigrationRequired leak risk
description: Returns passwordMigrationRequired flag — tells attackers which users can be phished via 'migrate your password' social engineering. Even with auth gate, the visibility rules may still leak (e.g. project-member-only visibility may be too broad).
suggested_fix: Drop passwordMigrationRequired from the response entirely (or move to admin-only endpoint with audit log). Always strip passwordHash, totpSecret, backupCodes, secondFactorPending.

title: Method check after auth
description: Method check at line 33 happens AFTER auth (line 28). Wasteful — should gate methods first to return 405 before session lookup.
suggested_fix: Move method check before getServerSession (or let withRoute handle it).

title: Selects isSystemAdmin and passwordMigrationRequired — info disclosure
description: Lines 46-47: `select: { isSystemAdmin: true, passwordMigrationRequired: true }` returned in response. Combined with API-176 finding.
suggested_fix: Strip from select. Or restrict to admin-only viewers with explicit check + audit log.

title: Public read endpoint, no auth, no withRoute
description: GET /api/types returns ALL work-package types to anyone (no auth). Likely intentional (these are reference data for forms), but inconsistent with project posture (everything else has auth).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute({ public: true, methods: ['GET'] }) for consistency. Consider auth-gating if types reveal internal taxonomy.

title: Public read endpoint, no auth
description: Same as API-179 for statuses.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute({ public: true, methods: ['GET'] }).

title: Public read endpoint, no auth
description: Same as API-179 for priorities.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute({ public: true, methods: ['GET'] }).

title: Public read endpoint leaks full role permissions
description: GET /api/roles returns ALL roles INCLUDING their `permissions` JSON array. This exposes the full RBAC taxonomy to any unauthenticated caller (info disclosure — an attacker now knows exactly what permission strings to try forging).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Either make this public but strip the permissions field from the response, OR require admin-only access to see permissions.

title: Raw handler — templates, no withRoute
description: Manual admin gate via JWT. zod for POST. No withRoute. No envelope. No audit log on POST (creating a template that affects all future projects from this template).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. bodySchema for POST. Audit log on template create.

title: Raw handler — template CRUD
description: Same pattern as API-183. PUT/PATCH has no zod enum for module names (accepts any strings).
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. bodySchema for PUT with module name validation against the ALL_MODULES enum.

title: Raw handler — OAuth settings, plaintext secrets in DB
description: Manual admin gate. GET returns googleClientSecret in plaintext (line 18). PUT stores googleClientSecret in plaintext (line 35). Same issue as LDAP bind passwords (API-59).
suggested_fix: Encrypt with libsodium/KMS before storing. Return a masked value in GET (e.g. '****XXXX' last 4 chars) — never return plaintext secret. Add audit log on secret rotation.

title: No envelope, no rate limit, no audit
description: Same withRoute migration benefits as other admin routes.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. bodySchema for PUT. Audit log on secret change. Rate limit on GET (admin-only PII dump).

title: Raw handler — WebAuthn register, no rate limit, `e: any`
description: Sensitive security endpoint (completes 2FA registration). No rate limit (could be spammed). `e: any` catch (line 50) leaks error.message-less envelope.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Add rate limit per userId (5 attempts/15min). Audit log on successful registration. Use typed Prisma error instead of any.

title: No body shape validation — pass-through to verifier
description: req.body is passed straight to verifyRegistration without schema validation. While the verifier will reject malformed payloads, doing zod pre-validation gives a clearer 400 error and prevents wasted verifier work.
suggested_fix: Add bodySchema: z.object({ id: z.string(), rawId: z.string(), response: z.object({ clientDataJSON: z.string(), attestationObject: z.string() }), type: z.string() }).

title: WebAuthn credential persist + totp status update not coordinated
description: If user already has totpEnabled=true and adds WebAuthn, the user's 2FA capabilities grow but totpEnabled stays true. If the user wants WebAuthn-only (no TOTP), there's no flag for that. Models/feature design gap.
suggested_fix: Add a webauthnEnabled flag on User model. On register, set webauthnEnabled=true. Login flow: require EITHER totp OR webauthn.

title: Returns enabled:true — misleading
description: Returns `{ success: true, enabled: true }` but the endpoint only registers a WebAuthn credential. There's no User.totpEnabled update here, so 'enabled' is ambiguous. Could mean 'credential enrolled' or '2FA enabled'.
suggested_fix: Return `{ success: true, credentialId: result.credentialId, webauthnEnabled: true }`. Be explicit about what was set.

title: Likely same RBAC gap as approve.ts (any authenticated user can reject)
description: By code-structure parallelism with approve.ts, this route almost certainly allows any logged-in user to reject any submitted time entry. Same CRITICAL severity as API-78.
suggested_fix: Read and audit reject.ts. Apply same fix as API-78: assertProjectMembership + TIME_ENTRY_APPROVE permission. Migrate to withRoute.

title: Raw handler — time-reports by-user, isAdmin via DB call
description: Same isAdmin-via-DB anti-pattern as API-143/167. Manual membership check. Manual envelope.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Use session.user.isSystemAdmin from JWT. Use assertProjectMembership helper.

title: Authorization only for userId !== viewerId
description: The auth check is conditional on `userId && userId !== viewerId`. If userId is not provided (or is empty), the where clause has no userId filter and returns ALL time entries the viewer has access to via workPackage.project — but the membership check is skipped. Edge case for query `?userId=` (empty string).
suggested_fix: Default userId to session.user.id when not provided. Or always run the authorization branch.

title: POSITIVE — proper withRoute usage with querySchema
description: withRoute HOF, zod querySchema with coerce/transform for pagination, ApiError thrown on bad input.
suggested_fix: No fix needed.

title: COVERAGE GAP — most raw handlers have no dedicated unit test
description: Of the 95 raw handlers, only the following have dedicated unit tests in __tests__/api/: documents, forums, meetings, projects, relations, wiki, work-packages (work-package-permissions, watch), announcements, work-package-permissions, work-packages-watch. 70+ raw handlers (groups, ldap, webhooks, custom-fields, files, time-entries/approve, time-entries/reject, queries, exports, settings/oauth, project-templates, admin/branding, my-page, notification-settings, sprints/*, budgets/*, repository/*, modules, wip-limits, copy, hard-delete already has withRoute, news, etc.) have NO dedicated test file.
suggested_fix: Add unit tests for each raw handler before migration to withRoute. Tests should cover: (1) auth-required 401, (2) RBAC permission denial 403, (3) zod validation 400, (4) happy path 200/201, (5) not-found 404. Use the existing __tests__/api/*.unit.test.ts patterns.

title: COVERAGE GAP — security-sensitive routes untested
description: Routes with critical security implications have no tests: time-entries/[id]/approve.ts, time-entries/[id]/reject.ts, auth/2fa/setup.ts, auth/2fa/verify.ts, users/[id]/2fa/disable.ts, users/[id]/2fa/webauthn-register-finish.ts, api-key.ts, ldap/* (all). Without tests, RBAC gaps (API-78, API-130, API-132) cannot be detected on regression.
suggested_fix: Priority: add tests for all 2FA flows (brute force resistance, re-auth on disable), all approval flows (RBAC), and all LDAP routes (admin gating). Reference the work-package-permissions.unit.test.ts pattern.

title: Integration test coverage is broad but shallow
description: routes-integration.test.ts likely tests happy paths across many routes. Need to verify it covers 401/403/400/404 paths for each route, not just 200. Without negative-path tests, security regressions slip through.
suggested_fix: Audit routes-integration.test.ts. Ensure every route has a 401 test (anon call), 403 test (authenticated but unauthorized), and 404 test (resource not found). Add 400 test for routes with zod bodySchema.

title: Uncaught throw in catch block (line 74) — repeated finding
description: `throw error` on line 74 propagates to the top level. The route has no outer try/catch. Returns default Next.js 500 with no envelope.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute — the HOF's catch block handles all uncaught errors uniformly.

title: zod regex for limit is correct but redundant
description: limit: z.string().regex(/^\d+$/) — parses to number via parseInt(query.limit, 10). Could use z.coerce.number() for cleaner code.
suggested_fix: Use z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(10).

title: API key auth path — no project-scoping either
description: Same as the session path: API key holders can list ALL projects. API keys should be scoped to a specific set of projects (or admin-only).
suggested_fix: Add a projectIds scope field to the ApiKey model. Validate the key's allowedProjects at validateApiKey time. Reject queries outside the allowed set.

title: isAllowedContentType check is good but no file-size enforcement at upload
description: Pre-signed URL allows contentType check + size claim from client, but actual upload to S3 cannot enforce size (S3 accepts the upload regardless). Add post-upload size verification + quota.
suggested_fix: After upload completes, GET HEAD on the object and verify Content-Length matches expected. Reject and delete the file if mismatch. Add per-user storage quota.

title: `as any` cast again — type safety regression
description: `(te as any).activity?.hourlyRate` — repeats API-94. Should use Prisma types.
suggested_fix: Update Prisma schema with the activity relation type, then use proper select/include. If activity.hourlyRate doesn't exist, that's the real bug — fix the schema.

title: safeParse followed by manual parse — partial migration
description: Line 32: parsed = disableSchema.safeParse(req.body), line 37: const { password, token, backupCode } = parsed.data. The safeParse is correct but the rest of the handler is still raw. No withRoute, no envelope.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute with bodySchema. Use ApiError throughout. Wrap the DB writes in transaction (API-23).

title: updateWorkPackageSchema accepts .cuid() fields but doesn't validate cuid format strictly
description: z.string().cuid() is correct. But assignment/due date validation allows date in past for already-completed WP. Domain rule gap.
suggested_fix: Add refinement: if statusId indicates 'closed' state, do not allow status change from closed to open without permission.

title: POST manual `if (!name)` validation
description: Line 23: `if (!name || !startDate || !endDate)` — no zod. Capacity parsed with parseFloat (line 29) — 'abc' becomes NaN.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. bodySchema: z.object({ name: z.string().min(1), startDate: z.coerce.date(), endDate: z.coerce.date(), capacity: z.coerce.number().positive().optional() }).refine(endDate >= startDate).

title: PATCH manual req.body destructuring
description: Same anti-pattern. status is accepted as any string.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. bodySchema: z.object({ name, startDate, endDate, capacity, status: z.enum(['planned','active','completed','cancelled']) }).

title: isSystemAdmin DB call on every branding GET
description: Branding is shown on every page load. The DB hit on every request is wasteful — session.user.isSystemAdmin is in JWT.
suggested_fix: Use session.user.isSystemAdmin from JWT. Migrate to withRoute.

title: JSON.parse without try/catch
description: Line 61: JSON.parse(user.backupCodes) — if backupCodes column is corrupted, throws and 500s without envelope.
suggested_fix: Wrap in try/catch. Return ApiError(500, 'CORRUPT_2FA_STATE', 'Backup codes column is corrupted; contact admin').

title: Body parsed with `as Record<string, unknown>` cast — type safety
description: Multiple `as` casts on lines 44-46. Use zod for safety.
suggested_fix: Define a zod schema for CSP report (union of legacy + new format) and use safeParse. skipSentryFor for invalid reports.

title: RBAC check bypassed — session.user.id !== userId but isSystemAdmin check is correct
description: Line 28: `session.user.id !== userId && !session.user.isSystemAdmin` — correct pattern (self-or-admin). But the 2FA disable needs additional re-auth (password + TOTP) per API-22 finding.
suggested_fix: Add password + TOTP check before disabling. Body schema must include these for the disable flow.

title: Disable 2FA without re-auth — critical security gap (repeat API-132)
description: Already documented in API-132 as critical. Listed again here as a flagged item to ensure prioritisation in the migration plan.
suggested_fix: Require password + TOTP in body for disable. Migrate to withRoute. Add audit log on disable.

title: Raw handler — v3 work-packages, no withRoute, no rate limit
description: Expensive query (joins work-package + project + status + type + assignee + priority). No rate limit. Could DoS by spamming large offset queries.
suggested_fix: Migrate to withRoute. Add per-user rate limit (30/min). Cap max(offset, 10000) to prevent deep pagination DoS. Use cursor-based pagination for the v3 API.

title: Lock user + delete links exposed in response
description: Line 68-69: response includes `_links.lockUser` and `_links.delete` for every user. OpenProject API spec includes these for admin operations. Even though admin-only, an admin user who pastes a link could inadvertently delete users. Consider scoping these to a separate user-detail endpoint with explicit delete lock intent.
suggested_fix: Add a query parameter (e.g. ?showLinks=true) that gates the destructive link exposure. Default to false.

title: Returns full user record incl. passwordMigrationRequired — info disclosure
description: Already documented in API-176/178. Listed again to emphasize: this endpoint leaks which users can be phished.
suggested_fix: Drop passwordMigrationRequired from select. Restrict to admins only (with audit log).

title: PATCH: any project member can edit any document — RBAC too permissive
description: PATCH only checks project membership; no document-specific permission. A 'Viewer' role with DOCUMENT_VIEW-only shouldn't be able to edit. But the schema only enforces membership.
suggested_fix: Add assertDocumentProjectMembership with 'DOCUMENT_EDIT' permission. Same for DELETE.

title: PATCH folder rename — any project member can rename
description: Same as API-215. Even view-only members can rename folders (changes other users' UI).
suggested_fix: Add assertFolderProjectMembership with 'FOLDER_EDIT' permission.

title: addMember — anyone can add members (RBAC gap, repeat API-10)
description: Already flagged in API-10 as critical. Listed again to ensure tracking.
suggested_fix: Add assertProjectMembership with 'MEMBER_CREATE' permission. Reject non-members.

title: Plain string CRON_SECRET compare — timing attack
description: Already documented in API-36 as medium. Listed again to ensure the cron route is included in the migration plan.
suggested_fix: Use crypto.timingSafeEqual for CRON_SECRET compare.

title: Includes attendees PII (email) — info disclosure for non-members
description: Phase 7 RBAC fix already gates non-members (assertProjectMembership). But the response includes full attendee email addresses — even project members might not need to see other members' emails in meeting context.
suggested_fix: Optionally strip email from attendees for non-admin viewers. Or accept this as design (project members can see each other's emails via member dashboard anyway).

title: POST mark-all-read updateMany not transactional with cache invalidation
description: Line 24: updateMany updates all unread to read. Line 28: returns success. If a Redis cache invalidation exists elsewhere (lib/cache/redis), it must run AFTER the DB commit.
suggested_fix: Wrap in transaction OR call setCachedUnreadCount(userId, 0) AFTER the DB write completes. Verify cache invalidation order.

1. API-78 / API-191 — time-entries/[id]/approve.ts and reject.ts: ANY authenticated user can approve/reject any time entry. CRITICAL RBAC gap (comment in approve.ts admits 'should be restricted by project membership'). Apply assertProjectMembership with TIME_ENTRY_APPROVE permission. ALSO API-132 + API-22 — auth/2fa/setup.ts disable path requires no re-auth, stolen session = 2FA disabled.

2. API-172/173 — pages/api/v3/work-packages.ts: parseFilters() injects user-controlled JSON directly into Prisma `where` object. No allowlist of filterable fields. Combined with no project-scoping, any authenticated user OR API key holder can list work packages across ALL projects. Fix: allowlist fields + add project-membership OR filter for non-admin viewers. ALSO API-115-119 — pages/api/projects/[projectId]/repository/* — shell-injection risk via `sha` param + arbitrary file read via localPath (CRITICAL: any user with auth can set localPath to any directory and GET the tree).

3. API-119 — pages/api/projects/[projectId]/repository/index.ts: arbitrary localPath on repository create = arbitrary file read via /repository/[repoId]/tree.ts. Plus the systematic Phase 1 RBAC hardening backlog: 95 raw handlers should migrate to withRoute. Phase 7 Sprint B-1/B-2 hardened 12 routes; the remaining 83 are mostly identical patterns (manual session, manual RBAC, manual envelope, no Sentry capture, no audit log, no rate limit on mutations). Prioritize the security-sensitive mutation routes (time-entries approve/reject, 2FA flows, ldap sync, webhooks CRUD, projects/copy, projects/hard-delete). Migrate in batches following the patterns in projects/[projectId]/index.ts (withRoute + ApiError + ApiErrorBody envelope + custom RBAC helper) and projects/[projectId]/meetings/* (withRoute + assertProjectMembership).
